project_summary.name: Aitri Hub — hub-folder-scan
project_summary.north_star_kpi: Time to register a full workspace of N projects drops from O(N) manual steps to 1 step
project_summary.jtbd: When I have a folder of multiple projects, I want to add them all at once so I can see every project card immediately without N separate admin actions
project_summary.guardrail_metric: Existing individually-registered projects must continue to collect and display without change

no_go_zone: No recursive scanning — only immediate children (depth=1) are scanned; nested workspaces are not supported
No file-system watcher — children are discovered on each collector cycle, not on inotify/FSEvents events
No remote folder types — folder scan is local filesystem only; remote URLs are not scannable
No merging of folder entries into projects.json — children are ephemeral, resolved dynamically by the collector, not persisted as individual entries
No UI for managing individual children of a folder — they cannot be edited or removed individually; only the parent folder entry is stored in projects.json
No depth-configurable scan — depth is always 1; deeper hierarchies require adding sub-folders manually

user_personas.name: Multi-project Developer
user_personas.role: Developer with 5–30 projects under a single workspace directory
user_personas.goal: See all project cards after one admin action
user_personas.pain_point: Currently must add each project individually even when they all live under /Projects

user_stories.i_want: to select 'folder' as the project type when adding a location in the admin panel
user_stories.so_that: I can register an entire workspace directory in one step

user_stories.i_want: the collector to expand folder entries into child cards
user_stories.so_that: I don't need to register each project manually

user_stories.i_want: only real project directories to appear as cards
user_stories.so_that: unrelated folders don't pollute the dashboard

user_stories.i_want: to get an error if the folder path I enter doesn't exist or isn't a directory
user_stories.so_that: I can fix the path before it silently produces zero cards

user_stories.i_want: to see a brief explanation when I pick 'folder' type
user_stories.so_that: I understand it scans immediate children only

user_stories.i_want: child project cards to carry a reference to their parent folder
user_stories.so_that: I can distinguish auto-discovered projects from manually-registered ones

functional_requirements.title: Admin panel — 'folder' project type option in Add Project form
acceptance_criteria: Given the user opens the Add Project form at /admin, when the type dropdown is expanded, then options include 'local', 'remote', and 'folder'

Given the user selects type='folder' and submits a valid absolute directory path, when POST /api/projects is called, then the server responds 201 and projects.json contains one entry with type='folder'

functional_requirements.title: Collector — folder-type projects expand into individual child project cards
acceptance_criteria: Given a registered project has type='folder' and location='/Projects', when the collector runs, then each valid immediate child directory of /Projects appears as a separate entry in dashboard.json
Given a folder-type project is registered, when dashboard.json is written, then the parent folder entry itself does NOT appear as a project card — only its discovered children appear

Given a folder-type project has 3 valid child directories, when the collector runs, then dashboard.json contains exactly 3 project entries derived from that folder (plus any other individually-registered projects)

functional_requirements.title: Collector — child validity check (package.json OR .aitri directory)
acceptance_criteria: Given a child directory contains a package.json file, when the validity check runs, then the child is included in collection as a valid project
Given a child directory contains a .aitri directory, when the validity check runs, then the child is included in collection as a valid project
Given a child directory contains neither package.json nor .aitri, when the validity check runs, then the child is skipped and does not appear in dashboard.json
Given a child path is a file (not a directory), when the validity check runs, then the file is skipped entirely

functional_requirements.title: Admin API — 'folder' type validation (path must exist and be a directory)
acceptance_criteria: Given POST /api/projects with type='folder' and a path that does not exist on disk, when the handler validates, then the response is 400 with error 'path_not_found'
Given POST /api/projects with type='folder' and a path that exists but is a file (not a directory), when the handler validates, then the response is 400 with error 'not_a_directory'

Given POST /api/projects with type='folder' and a valid existing directory path with no path traversal, when the handler validates, then the response is 201 and the entry is persisted with type='folder'

functional_requirements.title: Add Project form — helper text when type='folder' is selected
acceptance_criteria: Given the user selects type='folder' in the Add Project form, when the selection changes, then a helper text appears reading 'Scans immediate child directories — each one with package.json or .aitri appears as its own card'

functional_requirements.title: Collector — folder-derived child entries include parentFolder field in dashboard.json
acceptance_criteria: Given a child is discovered by scanning a folder-type project at location '/Projects', when the child entry is written to dashboard.json, then the entry includes a 'parentFolder' field with value '/Projects'
Given a project is manually registered with type='local', when its entry is written to dashboard.json, then no 'parentFolder' field is present on the entry

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Folder scan of a directory with up to 50 immediate children must complete within the 5-second collector cycle
acceptance_criteria: Given a folder-type project with 50 child directories each containing package.json, the scan completes in ≤500ms keeping total collection within the 5-second cycle

non_functional_requirements.requirement: A folder-type project whose location no longer exists must not crash the collector
acceptance_criteria: Given a folder-type project whose location has been deleted from disk, when the collector runs, the folder entry is silently skipped and all other registered projects continue to collect normally

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Folder scan must not follow symlinks that point outside the registered directory
acceptance_criteria: Given a symlink inside the folder points to a directory outside the registered location, when the collector scans children, the symlink target is skipped and no files outside the registered location are read

constraints: No new npm runtime dependencies — uses Node.js built-in fs module only
All persistent data written under ~/.aitri-hub/ — no writes to scanned project directories
